sections.label: Section 4.3.635
sections.original: Haec hinc facessat ; tu molestus ne sies .
sections.translation: so that she may betake herself off , and you annoy us no more ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.636
sections.translation: Are the Gods quite on good terms with him ? [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.636b
sections.translation: For I ’ m quite sure , if you were to mention any thing that ’ s fair and reasonable , as he is a reasonable man , you ’ ll not have to bandy three words with him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.639b
sections.translation: Who ordered you to say so ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.640
sections.translation: Nay , he could not have more happily contrived to bring about what we want .

sections.label: Section 4.3.641b
sections.translation: Undone !

sections.label: Section 4.3.641c
sections.translation: Go on with your story .

sections.label: Section 4.3.642
sections.translation: At first the fellow raved .

sections.label: Section 4.3.642b
sections.translation: Say , what did he ask ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.643
sections.translation: What ? A great deal too much .

sections.label: Section 4.3.643b
sections.translation: How much ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.643c
sections.translation: Suppose he were to give a great talent .

sections.label: Section 4.3.644b
sections.original: Immo malum hercle : ut nihil pudet .
sections.translation: Aye , faith , perdition to him rather ; has he no shame ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.645
sections.original: Quod dixi adeo ei . Quaeso quid si filiam
sections.translation: Just what I said to him : Pray , said I , suppose he was portioning an only daughter of his own . It has been of little benefit that he hasn ’ t one of his own , when another has been found to be demanding a fortune . To be brief , and to pass over his impertinences , this at last was his final answer :

sections.label: Section 4.3.650
sections.original: Ego , inquit , iam a principio amici filiam ,
sections.translation: I , said he , from the very first , have been desirous to marry the daughter of my friend , as was fit I should ; for I was aware of the ill results of this , a poor wife being married into a rich family , and becoming a slave . But , as I am now conversing with you unreservedly ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.655
sections.translation: I was in want of a wife to bring me a little money with which to pay off my debts ; and even yet , if Demipho is willing to give as much as I am to receive with her to whom I am engaged , there is no one whom I should better like for a wife .

sections.label: Section 4.3.659
sections.original: Utrum stultitia facere ego hunc an malitia
sections.translation: Whether to say he ’ s doing this through folly or mischief ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.660
sections.original: Dicam , scientem an imprudentem , incertus sum .
sections.translation: through stupidity or design , I ’ m in doubt .

sections.label: Section 4.3.661
sections.translation: What if he ’ s in debt to the amount of his life ? [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.661b
sections.translation: His land is mortgaged ,— for ten minae he said .

sections.label: Section 4.3.662b
sections.translation: Well , well , let him take her then ; I ’ ll give it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.663
sections.original: Aediculae item sunt ob decem alias .
sections.translation: He has a house besides , mortgaged for another ten .

sections.label: Section 4.3.663b
sections.translation: Huy , huy ! that ’ s too much .

sections.label: Section 4.3.664b
sections.original: Ne clama : petito hasce a me decem .
sections.translation: Don ’ t be crying out ; you may have those ten of me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.665
sections.original: Uxori emenda ancillula est : tum pluscula
sections.translation: A lady ’ s maid must be brought for his wife ; and then too , a little more is wanted for some furniture , and some is wanted for the wedding expenses . Well then , said he , for these items , put down ten more .

sections.label: Section 4.3.668
sections.original: Sexcentas proinde scribito iam mihi dicas .
sections.translation: Then let him at once bring six hundred actions [*] against me ; I shall give nothing at all ; is this dirty fellow to be laughing at me as well ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.670
sections.original: Quaeso , ego dabo , quiesce : tu modo filius
sections.translation: Pray do be quiet ; I ’ ll give it : do you only bring your son to marry the woman we want him to have .

sections.label: Section 4.3.671b
sections.translation: Ah me ! Geta , you have ruined me by your treachery .

sections.label: Section 4.3.673
sections.original: Mea causa eiicitur : me hoc est aequum amittere .
sections.translation: ’ Tis on my account she ’ s turned off ; it ’ s right that I should bear the loss .

sections.label: Section 4.3.674
sections.original: Quantum potest me certiorem , inquit , face ;
sections.translation: Take care and let me know , said he , as soon as possible ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.675
sections.original: Si illam dant , hanc ut mittam ; ne incertus siem ;
sections.translation: if they are going to let me have her , that I may get rid of the other , so that I mayn ’ t be in doubt ; for the others have agreed to . pay me down the portion directly .

sections.label: Section 4.3.677
sections.translation: Let him have her at once ; let him give notice to them that he breaks off the match with the other , and let him marry this woman .

sections.label: Section 4.3.678b
sections.original: Quae quidem illi res vertat male .
sections.translation: Yes , and little joy to him of the bargain !

sections.label: Section 4.3.679
sections.original: Opportune adeo argentum nunc mecum attuli ,
sections.translation: Luckily , too , I ’ ve now brought home some money with me ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.680
sections.original: Fructum quem Lemni uxoris reddunt praedia :
sections.translation: the rents which my wife ’ s farms at Lemnos produce . I ’ ll take it out of that , and tell my wife that you had occasion for it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.682
sections.translation: Geta .

sections.label: Section 4.4.682b
sections.translation: Well .

sections.label: Section 4.4.682c
sections.translation: What have you been doing ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.682d
sections.translation: Diddling the old fellows out of their money .

sections.label: Section 4.4.683
sections.translation: Is that quite the thing ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.683b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t know : it ’ s just what I was told to do .

sections.label: Section 4.4.684
sections.original: Eho , verbero , aliud mihi respondes ac rogo ?
sections.translation: How now , whip-scoundrel , do you give me an answer to what I don ’ t ask you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.685
sections.translation: What was it then that you did ask ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.685b
sections.translation: What was it I did ask ? Through your agency , matters have most undoubtedly come to the pass that I may go hang myself . May then all the Gods , Goddesses , Deities above and below , with every evil confound you ! Look now , if you wish any thing to succeed , intrust it to him who may bring you from smooth water on to a rock .

sections.label: Section 4.4.690
sections.original: Quid minus utibile fuit quam hoc ulcus tangere ,
sections.translation: What was there less advantageous than to touch upon this sore , or to name my wife ? Hopes have been excited in my father that she may possibly be got rid of . Pray now , tell me , suppose Phormio receives the portion , she must be taken home by him as his wife : what ’ s to become of me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.694b
sections.translation: But he ’ s not going to marry her .

sections.label: Section 4.4.694c
sections.translation: I know that . But ( ironically )

sections.label: Section 4.4.695
sections.original: Quum argentum repetent , nostra causa scilicet
sections.translation: when they demand the money back , of course , for our sake , he ’ ll prefer going to prison .

sections.label: Section 4.4.696b
sections.translation: There is nothing , Antipho , but what it may be made worse by being badly told : you leave out what is good , and you mention the bad . Now then , hear the other side : if he receives the money ,

sections.label: Section 4.4.700
sections.original: Ducenda est uxor , ut ais : concedo tibi .
sections.translation: she must be taken as his wife , you say ; I grant you ; still , some time at least will be allowed for preparing for the nuptials , for inviting , and for sacrificing . In the mean time , Phoedria ’ s friends will advance what they have promised ; out of that he will repay it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.704b
sections.translation: On what grounds ? Or what will he say ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.704c
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.705
sections.original: Quot res postilla monstra evenerunt mihi !
sections.translation: How many circumstances , since then , have befallen me as prodigies ? A strange black dog [*] entered the house ; a snake came down from the tiles through the sky-light ; [*] a hen crowed ; [*] the soothsayer forbade it ; the diviner [*] warned me not : besides , before winter

sections.label: Section 4.4.710
sections.original: Negoti incipere ; quae causa est iustissima .
sections.translation: there is no sufficient reason for me to commence upon any new undertaking . This will be the case .

sections.label: Section 4.4.711b
sections.translation: I only wish it may be the case .

sections.label: Section 4.4.711c
sections.translation: It shall be the case ; trust me for that . Your father ’ s coming out ; go tell Phaedria that the money is found .

sections.label: Section 4.5.713
sections.original: Quietus esto , inquam : ego curabo ne quid verborum duit .
sections.translation: Do be quiet , I tell you ; I ’ ll take care he shall not be playing any tricks upon us . I ’ ll not rashly part with this without having my witnesses ;

sections.label: Section 4.5.715
sections.original: Cui dem , et quamobrem dem , commemorabo .
sections.translation: I ’ ll have it stated to whom I pay it , and for what purpose I pay it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.715b
sections.original: Ut cautus est , ubi nihil opus est !
sections.translation: How cautious he is , when there ’ s no need for it !

sections.label: Section 4.5.716
sections.original: Atqui ita opus facto est ; at matura , dum libido eadem haec manet ;
sections.translation: Why yes , you had need do so , and with all haste , while the fit is upon him ; for if this other woman shall prove more pressing , perhaps he may throw us over .
